title: dactylorhiza × braunii nothosubsp. lilacina
intro: Dactylorhiza × braunii nothosubsp. lilacina, also known as Braun's orchid, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza brennensis and Dactylorhiza traunsteineri. This plant is reported to have medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Braun's orchid include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza × braunii nothosubsp. lilacina may be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is necessary to fully understand its therapeutic applications and potential side effects.
